essence: Assesses buildings for fire risks, identifies hazards, and recommends measures to ensure safety and legal compliance.

identity.title: Fire Risk Assessor
identity.summary: Conducts fire‑risk assessments for homes, businesses, and public buildings, evaluating hazards, escape routes, fire‑protection systems, and compliance with regulations.

salary.notes: Highly qualified assessors or those handling complex commercial portfolios can exceed £65k-£80k+.

qualifications: Fire‑risk assessment training (e.g., NEBOSH, FPA, or equivalent)
Knowledge of fire‑safety legislation and building regulations
Ability to identify hazards and recommend mitigation measures
Understanding of passive and active fire‑protection systems

lifestyle.description: Fire risk assessors visit buildings to evaluate fire hazards, escape routes, fire‑protection systems, and compliance with regulations. They produce detailed reports and advise clients on safety improvements. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigative work, regulatory interpretation, and contributing to life‑safety compliance.

habits: Identifies fire hazards and evaluates risks
Reviews escape routes and fire‑protection systems
Communicates clearly with building managers and clients
